Skilled Nursing Facility Strategic Benchmark Report

Client-specific static report from CMS SNF cost-report metrics

Executive summary

THATCHER BROOK REHAB CENTER | CCN 465169 | CLEARFIELD, UT | DAVIS County
Overall read: occupancy is broadly in line with similar peers; net patient revenue per resident day exceeds operating expense per resident day; patient service margin is positive; liquidity appears thin based on days cash on hand.

Facility: THATCHER BROOK REHAB CENTER
CCN: 465169
Location: CLEARFIELD, UT; DAVIS County
Latest cost-report year: 2023
Peer group: Same state + same rural/urban + same bed-size band; n = 17.

This report summarizes cost-report-based strategic indicators for the selected skilled nursing facility. It is designed as a first-pass consulting report, not a substitute for audited financial review, facility interviews, staffing data, quality measures, or referral-market analysis.

  • Latest occupancy is 88.2%, compared with a peer median of 85.9%; the three-year change is up 25.8% from 2020 to 2023.
  • Medicare day share is 44.2% and Medicaid day share is not available, giving an initial view of post-acute versus long-stay payer mix.
  • Net patient revenue per resident day is $479, while operating expense per resident day is $464.
  • Patient service margin is 2.9%, compared with a peer median of -1.3%.
  • Contract labor ratio was not available or could not be calculated from the cost-report fields.

How to read this report

  • Peer comparisons use the selected facility’s latest-year peer group: same state, same rural/urban category, and same bed-size band.
  • Trend charts compare the target facility with the latest-year peer group over time. The shaded band is the peer 25th to 75th percentile range; the dashed line is the peer median; the solid line is the target facility.
  • Cost-report metrics are screening indicators. They are useful for identifying likely issues and follow-up questions, but they should be validated with staffing, quality, survey, referral, rate, and management information before making operational recommendations.

Rule-based strategic flags

Watch · Utilization
Occupancy has declined
Occupancy is down more than five percentage points from roughly three years earlier.
Strength · Revenue
Revenue per day is above peers
Net patient revenue per resident day is above the peer median.
Watch · Cost
Operating expense is high
Operating expense per resident day is above the peer 75th percentile.
Watch · Labor
Contract labor reliance is elevated
Contract labor ratio is above the peer 75th percentile or above 10%.
Watch · Labor
Total labor cost per day is high
Combined salary, wage-related, and contract labor cost per resident day is above the peer 75th percentile.
Watch · Labor
Labor share of expense is high
Broad labor cost share of operating expense is above the peer 75th percentile.
Risk · Liquidity
Liquidity appears thin
Days cash on hand is below the peer 25th percentile or below 30 days.

Trend analysis

Trend charts show the selected facility against the latest-year peer group where peer comparison is available. Payer mix and revenue-versus-expense charts are target operating views intended to show how the facility’s own business model has changed over time.

Occupancy

Interpretation: Occupancy is the clearest capacity-utilization signal in the cost report. A target line below the peer median suggests census pressure; a target line above peers suggests stronger utilization or a different care model.

Payer mix

Interpretation: Medicare share is a rough proxy for skilled/post-acute orientation, while Medicaid share often reflects long-stay reimbursement exposure. Directional change can matter as much as the latest value.

Revenue and operating expense per resident day

Interpretation: If operating expense per day is growing faster than revenue per day, the facility may be facing margin compression even if occupancy is stable.

Patient service margin

Interpretation: Patient service margin shows whether resident-care operations appear financially sustainable based on cost-report fields. A persistent negative margin requires deeper review of payer mix, labor cost, overhead, occupancy, and accounting treatment.

Contract labor ratio

Interpretation: Contract labor ratio is a screening indicator for agency or contracted labor reliance. A high or rising value may suggest staffing instability, wage pressure, or temporary surge staffing, but it should be validated with PBJ staffing data.

Consulting implications and next questions

The cost report points to financial, utilization, payer-mix, labor, and liquidity patterns. Before making operational recommendations, the next consulting step should validate these findings with additional sources:

  • PBJ staffing data for RN/LPN/CNA staffing and agency reliance.
  • Care Compare quality, survey, and deficiency data.
  • Local hospital discharge and referral-market context.
  • Medicaid rate and managed care context.
  • Leadership interview or facility operating review.

Recommended follow-up questions

  1. Occupancy has declined
    Is the occupancy pattern driven by hospital referral volume, staffing constraints, quality/reputation issues, discharge patterns, or local market demand?
  2. Operating expense is high
    Is the financial pressure driven by payer mix, labor cost, case mix, overhead, occupancy, or accounting treatment?
  3. Contract labor reliance is elevated
    Is contract labor reliance temporary, seasonal, or a structural staffing problem, and how does it compare with PBJ staffing levels and turnover?
  4. Total labor cost per day is high
    Is contract labor reliance temporary, seasonal, or a structural staffing problem, and how does it compare with PBJ staffing levels and turnover?
  5. Labor share of expense is high
    Is contract labor reliance temporary, seasonal, or a structural staffing problem, and how does it compare with PBJ staffing levels and turnover?

Source: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), Healthcare Cost Report Information System (HCRIS), Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report public-use data (CMS-2540-10). This report uses cleaned Utah facility cost-report years 2011–2023 from CMS source files spanning 2010–2024. Cost-report metrics are unaudited screening indicators and should be validated with staffing, quality, survey, referral-market, rate, and management information before making operational recommendations.
